name: importlab

metadata.name: importlab
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A library to calculate python dependency graphs.

documentation:
# importlab

Importlab is a library for Python that automatically infers dependencies
and calculates a dependency graph. It can perform dependency ordering of
a set of files, including cycle detection.

Importlab's main use case is to work with static analysis tools that
process one file at a time, ensuring that a file's dependencies are
analysed before it is.

(This is not an official Google product.)

# License

Apache 2.0

# Installation

Importlab can be installed from pip

```
pip install importlab
```

To check out and install the latest source code

```
git clone https://github.com/google/importlab.git
cd importlab
python setup.py install
```

# Usage

Importlab is primarily intended to be used as a library. It takes one or
more python files as arguments, and generates an import graph, typically
used to process files in dependency order.

It is currently integrated into
[pytype](https://github.com/google/pytype)

# Command-line tool

Importlab ships with a small command-line tool, also called
`importlab`, which can display some information about a project's
import graph.

```
usage: importlab [-h] [--tree] [--unresolved] [filename [filename ...]]

positional arguments:
  filename              input file(s)

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --tree                Display import tree.
  --unresolved          Display unresolved dependencies.
```

# Roadmap

- `Makefile` generation, to take advantage of `make`'s incremental
  update and parallel execution features
- Integration with other static analysis tools

modules.name: importlab.utils
documentation: Utility functions.

functions.name: importlab.utils.collect_files
documentation: Collect all the files with extension in a directory tree.

functions.name: importlab.utils.expand_source_files
documentation:
Expand a list of filenames passed in as sources.

This is a helper function for handling command line arguments that specify a
list of source files and directories.

Any directories in filenames will be scanned recursively for .py files.
Any files that do not end with ".py" will be dropped.

Args:
    filenames: A list of filenames to process.
    cwd: An optional working directory to expand relative paths
Returns:
    A list of sorted full paths to .py files

functions.name: importlab.utils.makedirs
documentation: Create a nested directory; don't fail if any of it already exists.

functions.name: importlab.utils.strip_suffix
documentation: Remove a suffix from a string if it exists.

classes.name: importlab.parsepy.ParseError
documentation: Error parsing a file with python.

classes.name: importlab.parsepy.ImportStatement

methods.name: importlab.parsepy.ImportStatement.__new__
documentation:
Create a new ImportStatement.

Args:
  name: Name of the module to be imported. E.g. "sys".
  new_name: What the module is renamed to. The "y" in
    "import x as y".
  is_from: If the last part of the name (the "z" in "x.y.z") can
  be an element within a module, instead of a module itself. Happens
  e.g. for "from sys import argv".
  is_star: If this is an import of the form "from x import *".
  source: The path to the file as resolved by python.
Returns:
  A new ImportStatement instance.
A Python import statement, such as "import foo as bar".

functions.name: importlab.resolve.convert_to_path
documentation: Converts ".module" to "./module", "..module" to "../module", etc.

functions.name: importlab.resolve.infer_module_name
documentation: Convert a python filename to a module relative to pythonpath.

functions.name: importlab.resolve.get_absolute_name
documentation:
Joins a package name and a relative name.

Args:
  package: A dotted name, e.g. foo.bar.baz
  relative_name: A dotted name with possibly some leading dots, e.g. ..x.y

Returns:
  The relative name appended to the parent's package, after going up one
    level for each leading dot.
      e.g. foo.bar.baz + ..hello.world -> foo.hello.world
  The unchanged relative_name if it does not start with a dot
    or has too many leading dots.

classes.name: importlab.resolve.Direct
documentation: Files added directly as arguments.

classes.name: importlab.resolve.Builtin
documentation: Imports that are resolved via python's builtins.

classes.name: importlab.resolve.System
documentation: Imports that are resolved by python.

classes.name: importlab.resolve.Local
documentation: Imports that are found in a local pythonpath.

methods.name: importlab.resolve.Resolver.resolve_import
documentation:
Simulate how Python resolves imports.

Returns the filename of the source file Python would load
when processing a statement like 'import name' in the module
we're currently under.

Args:
    item: An instance of ImportItem

Returns:
    A filename

Raises:
    ImportException: If the module doesn't exist.

methods.name: importlab.resolve.Resolver.resolve_all
documentation:
Resolves a list of imports.

Yields filenames.

functions.name: importlab.environment.path_from_pythonpath
documentation: Create an fs.Path object from a pythonpath string.

classes.name: importlab.graph.NodeSet
documentation: A strongly connected component - a set of mutually dependent files.

classes.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.add_file
documentation: Add a file and all its immediate dependencies to the graph.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.follow_file
documentation: Whether to recurse into a file's dependencies.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.add_file_recursive
documentation:
Add a file and all its recursive dependencies to the graph.

Args:
  filename: The name of the file.
  trim: Whether to trim the dependencies of builtin and system files.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.shrink_to_node
documentation: Shrink a strongly connected component into a node.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.build
documentation: Finalise the graph, after adding all input files to it.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.sorted_source_files
documentation: Returns a list of targets in topologically sorted order.

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.deps_list
documentation: Returns a list of (target, dependencies).

methods.name: importlab.graph.DependencyGraph.get_all_unresolved
documentation: Returns a set of all unresolved imports.

A set of file dependencies stored in a graph structure.

The graph needs to be constructed in two phases:
1. Call add_file_recursive() for every root file to add to the graph.
2. Call build() to collapse cycles and build the final graph.

Calling build() sets self.final = True and treats the graph as immutable
thereafter.

classes.name: importlab.graph.ImportGraph

methods.name: importlab.graph.ImportGraph.create
documentation:
Create and return a final graph.

Args:
  env: An environment.Environment object
  filenames: A list of filenames
  trim: Whether to trim the dependencies of builtin and system files.

Returns:
  An immutable ImportGraph with the recursive dependencies of all the
  files in filenames

methods.name: importlab.graph.ImportGraph.get_source_file_provenance
documentation: Infer the module name if possible.
A dependency graph built from file imports.

modules.name: importlab.import_finder
documentation: NOTE: Do not add any dependencies to this file - it needs to be run in a
subprocess by a python version that might not have any installed packages,
including importlab itself.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder._find_package
documentation: Helper function for _resolve_import_versioned.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder._resolve_import_versioned
documentation: Python 2 helper function for resolve_import.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder._resolve_import_versioned
documentation: Python 3 helper function for resolve_import.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder._resolve_import
documentation: Helper function for resolve_import.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder.resolve_import
documentation:
Use python to resolve an import.

Args:
  name: The fully qualified module name.

Returns:
  The path to the module source file or None.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder.get_imports
documentation:
Get all the imports in a file.

Each import is a tuple of:
  (name, alias, is_from, is_star, source_file)

functions.name: importlab.import_finder.print_imports
documentation: Print imports in csv format to stdout.

functions.name: importlab.import_finder.read_imports
documentation: Print imports in csv format to stdout.

documentation: Walk an AST collecting import statements.

methods.name: importlab.fs.FileSystem.relative_path
documentation:
Return the relative path to `path`.

If this filesystem has a root directory, and path is within that
directory tree, return the relative path; otherwise return None.
Interface for file systems.
